headline: UBS Appoints Daniele Magazzeni as Chief AI Officer to Lead Firm-Wide AI Strategy
the_so_what: UBS creating a dedicated Chief AI Officer role — hiring from JPMorgan, reporting to the Group COO, and overseeing 300+ live AI use cases — formalises AI as a C-suite function at the world's largest wealth manager, setting the governance template that boards at other global private banks will be asked to replicate.
summary: UBS has appointed Daniele Magazzeni as Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer, effective 1 January 2026, to advance the firm's AI strategy and increase adoption of new technologies. Magazzeni joins from J.P. Morgan, where he served as Chief Analytics Officer for the EMEA region and the Commercial and Investment Bank, and previously was Associate Professor of Artificial Intelligence at King's College London. He will lead the Chief Artificial Intelligence Office, overseeing deployment of AI-powered tools at scale, maintaining standards, and building technological foundations across the organization. Magazzeni will report to Mike Dargan, Group Chief Operations and Technology Officer, and be based in London. UBS currently has over 300 live AI use cases and is rolling out AI-powered tools to all employees, including M365 Copilot and its in-house AI Assistant Red.

key_stat.label: live AI use cases at UBS

_governance.notes: The core appointment details are fully verified against the source. However, the source text is truncated (ends mid-sentence about 'Big Rocks' initiatives), preventing verification of the '300+ live AI use cases' statistic and the specific AI tools mentioned (M365 Copilot, 'Red' AI Assistant). These claims may exist further in the article but cannot be confirmed from the available excerpt. No claims directly contradict the source.
